title: Enrollment Failed
description: Detects when the Autopilot enrollment has explicitly failed.
explanation:
The Autopilot enrollment has explicitly failed. The most specific failure information is carried by the events captured during this session — review the `enrollment_failed` event payload and the more targeted rule results (ANALYZE-APP-* / ANALYZE-ESP-*) that fired alongside this one for the actionable root cause.

Key facts extracted from the timeline:

- **Failure reason:** `{{reason}}`
- **ESP failed subcategory:** `{{failedSubcategory}}`
- **ESP HRESULT:** `{{errorCode}}`

(Fields shown as `{{…}}` were not present in this session — e.g. a non-ESP failure path has no failed-subcategory.)

remediation.title: Investigate enrollment failure
steps: Read the `reason` field on the enrollment_failed event for the immediate trigger (e.g. esp_terminal_failure, hello_timeout, max_lifetime)

If a HRESULT is present, look it up against the ESP error catalog (Microsoft Learn: Autopilot ESP error codes) — the ANALYZE-APP-* rules in this session correlate specific HRESULTs to actionable root causes (e.g. 0x87D1041C → detection rule mismatch)
Review other analyze rule results for correlated issues (disk space, network, app failures)
Check the device event timeline for the sequence of events leading to failure
Verify the Autopilot profile and ESP configuration in Intune
